summary: ecto-nightly-spirit postinstall exfiltrates AWS secrets, env vars, and CTF flags to attacker webhook
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js auto-executes a credential-theft and data-exfiltration payload against the installer's host. The script reads /app/flag.txt, greps the filesystem for CTF flag patterns, dumps git history, enumerates AWS Secrets Manager across us-east-1/eu-west-1/eu-central-1 via `aws secretsmanager list-secrets` and `get-secret-value`, runs `aws sts get-caller-identity`, and captures the full process environment via `env | sort`. The collected blob is POSTed to https://webhook.site/602a4c72-7033-4e28-92ea-dc66e59206e5 and PUT to hardcoded attacker IPs http://154.57.164.82:31250 and http://154.57.164.71:31289. Execution is gated by a 12-hex-character hostname check and a /app/node_modules cwd check — typical container/CTF targeting. Shell execution is performed through `execFileSync('/bin/sh', ['-c', cmd])` in the lifecycle hook, so the harm fires automatically without any user action beyond installing the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
